name: citation-management
tagline: Searches Google Scholar/PubMed, extracts metadata (DOI/PMID/arXiv), and builds/validates BibTeX bibliographies.
verdict: No security exploit found — API keys are used only for their stated purpose. Main risks are hygiene/quality: the file is ~2.5x the size budget, a 'MANDATORY' phase silently depends on an undeclared 'parallel-cli' tool, an unrelated schematics upsell creeps into scope, and forceful language pushes arbitrary citation-count quotas that risk padding a bibliograp

highlights.summary: Heavy imperative language ('MANDATORY' line 216, 'Non-Negotiable' line 496, 'NEVER' line 997, 'CRITICAL' line 585) plus a hard-coded citation-count quota table ('Citations must always be high in numbe
The 'Visual Enhancement with Scientific Schematics' section (lines 32-62) instructs always generating AI diagrams via a separate scientific-schematics skill/'Nano Banana Pro' for any document — scope
No 'do not use when' / negative-trigger guidance appears anywhere in the file.
